scenarios.opening_query: The IRS put a lien on our house over back taxes and my wife found out from a mortgage refinance denial.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We already went through appeals and lost — the collection due process determination is final, and I'm told the next stop is Tax Court on a strict deadline.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Appeals is over and we lost — the CDP determination is final, and Tax Court on a strict deadline is what's left.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether administrative remedies are exhausted forcing Tax Court
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We never responded to anything — no hearing was ever requested, so the work is reopening collection alternatives with the IRS directly.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We never responded to any notices — so it's about reopening things with the IRS directly.
scenarios.vetting_note: Exhaustion posture separates IRS negotiation from Tax Court litigation.

scenarios.opening_query: My food cart got a huge tax bill I don't understand and the state says pay first, argue later.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Taxation > Personal Income || Taxation > Business & Corporate
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a business assessment — the state reconstructed my cart's sales from card data and assessed transit and business taxes against the LLC.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's against the business — they reconstructed the cart's sales from card data and assessed the LLC.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the assessment targets the business entity
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my personal return — they disallowed my home-office and vehicle deductions from the cart years.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my personal return — disallowed home-office and vehicle deductions.
scenarios.vetting_note: Entity-vs-personal assessment behind one 'tax bill' framing.

scenarios.opening_query: I just discovered my late husband hadn't filed our taxes for six years. I'm terrified of what happens next.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Taxation > Personal Income || Wills & Trusts > Probate
scenarios.hidden_fact: His estate is open with me as representative — the six years of returns are the estate's obligation to file, and the probate can't close until they're resolved.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His estate is open and I'm the representative — the missing returns are the estate's to file before probate can close.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether estate administration frames the tax cleanup
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no estate — everything passed to me automatically; the exposure is MY innocent-spouse position on those joint years.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No estate to open — everything passed to me. It's my innocent-spouse exposure on the joint years.
scenarios.vetting_note: Estate-open fact routes the same discovery to probate practice.

scenarios.opening_query: I do gig work on three apps and the tax people say I owe self-employment money I never knew about.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Taxation > Personal Income || Labor & Employment > FLSA - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: One 'app' is really a delivery company that controls my shifts, routes, and uniform — a lawyer told me I'm likely a misclassified employee, which changes both the taxes and my rights.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: One company controls my shifts, routes, and uniform — I'm told I'm likely a misclassified employee, which changes the taxes and my rights.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether worker misclassification underlies the tax bill
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The gigs are genuinely independent — the issue is I never made quarterly payments and the penalties snowballed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The gigs are genuinely independent — I just never paid quarterlies and penalties snowballed.
scenarios.vetting_note: Misclassification fact converts a tax-debt case into an employment-rights case.
